heading: Update your Federated Database Instance Configuration
body:
To update your federated database instance configuration, connect to your instance and use the
following command:```javascript
use admin
db.runCommand( { "storageSetConfig" : <config> } )
```Replace `<config>` with the configuration for the federated database instance.> **Tip:** MongoDB recommends you to [Validate Configuration File](adf-validatestorageconfig) your
> Federated Database Instance configuration before applying any changes.To update your Federated Database Instance configuration Data stores, data sources, databases,
collections, and views, follow these steps:* For a visual interface, click Visual.

* For editing directly in the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) file, click JSON Editor.<Tab tabid="visual">
  Visual Editor

  ## Manage Data Sources

  ### Add an Additional Data Source

  Corresponds to `stores` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

  - Click Add Data Store Sources.

  - Select the data source provider you want to configure:
    \- For AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service), continue to step 5 of [Create AWS Data Store From the UI](deploy-s3).
    \- For Google Cloud, continue to step 7 of [Deploy a Federated Database Instance Data Store](deploy-gcp).
    \- For Azure (Microsoft Azure), continue to step 7 of [Create Azure Data Store From the UI](deploy-azure).
    \- For Atlas Cluster, continue to step 7 of [Create Atlas Data Store From the UI](deploy-atlas-datastore).
    \- For HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) data store, continue to step 7 of [Create Atlas Data Store From the UI](deploy-atlas-datastore).
    \- For Atlas Online Archive, continue to step 7 of [Create Online Archive Data Store From the UI](deploy-oa-datastore).

  ### Edit Your Data Source Name

  Corresponds to `stores.[n].name` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

  - Select your data store source from the list on the left.

  - Click the **Edit Store Name**  icon.

  - Change the current name in the **Data Store Name** field.

  - Click **Done**.

  ### Delete a Data Source

  - Select your Data Store source from the list on the left

  - Click the **Delete this Store**  icon

  - Click the **Delete** button.

  **Manage Databases**

  ### Create a New Database

  Corresponds to `databases` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

  - Click the **Add Database** button on the right-top corner

  - Fill in the **Database Name** field

  - Fill in the **Collection Name** field

  - Click the **Create** button

  ### Edit a Database name

  Corresponds to `databases.[n].name` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

  - Select your Database from the list.

  - Click the **Edit Database Name**  icon.

  - Update the **Database Name**.

  - Click the **Done** button.

  ### Delete a Database

  - Select your Database from the list.

  - Click **Delete this Database**  icon.

  - Click the **Delete** button.

  **Manage Collections**

  ### Create a new Collection in a Database

  Corresponds to `databases.[n].collections` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

  - Select the Database from where you want to create a Collection

  - Click the **Add Collection**  icon

  - Fill in the **Collection Name** field

  - Click the **Create** button

  ### Edit a Collection name

  Corresponds to `databases.[n].collections.[n].name` JSON (Javascript Object Notation)
  configuration setting.

  - Select your Collection from the list

  - Click the **Edit Collection Name**  icon

  - Change the current name in the **Collection Name** field for your new name

  - Click the **Create** button

  ### Delete a Collection

  - Select your Collection from the list

  - Click the **Delete this Collection**  icon

  - Click the **Delete** button.

  **Manage Views**

  ### Create a new [View](manual/core/views) in a Collection

  Corresponds to `databases.[n].views` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

  - Select the Collection from where you want to create a View

  - Click the **Add View**  icon

  - Fill in the **View Name** field

  - Fill in the **:manual:\`Pipeline \</core/aggregation-pipeline/>\`** field

  - Click the **Create** button

  ### Edit a View name or Pipeline

  Corresponds to `databases.[n].views.[n].name` and
  `databases.[n].views.[n].pipeline` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration settings
  respectively.

  - Select your View from your Collection

  - Click the **Edit View Name**  icon

  - Change the current name in the **View Name** field for your new name

  - Change the current pipeline in the **Pipeline** field for your new pipeline

  - Click the **Save** button

  ### Delete a View

  - Select your View from the list

  - Click the **Delete this View**  icon

  - Click the **Delete** button.
</Tab>JSON EditorEdit the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration file objects to add, create, or remove
`stores`, `databases`, or `views`To learn more about the configuration settings of your specific Data Store
provider, see their Configuration File page:- [Configure Atlas Data Federation to query data from S3](adf-configuration-file-aws)

- [Azure data stores](adf-configuration-file-azure)

- [Google Cloud Storage Bucket](adf-configuration-file-gcp)

- [Atlas cluster](adf-configuration-file-atlas)

- [HTTP URL](adf-configuration-file-http)

- [Online Archives](adf-configuration-file-oa)mongoshTo learn how to set and manage storage configuration using
[MongoDB Shell (mongosh)](mongodb-shell), see the page
[view](adf-cli).
